content.full_text:
Copy also filed: 18225

March 25, 1981

N. Manfred Shaffer
Governmental Education and Research
P.O. Box 1293
Santa Cruz, California 95061

A-81-03-111

Dear Mr. Shaffer:

Thank you for your letter which was received by the Commission
on March 15, 1981, requesting advice regarding the obligation of the
Governmental Education and Research Association to file campaign
disclosure statements under provisions of the Political Reform Act
of 1974.

The facts as I understand them are that the Governmental
Education and Research Association (GEAR) is a non-profit association
of business and professional people in Santa Cruz County. The
association was formed generally to study local government issues,
to report upon such issues to the members of the association, to
formulate positions on such issues, and to inform the public of its
position on such issues. GEAR will not be providing any services
to local candidates. Furthermore, the association will attend
meetings of government bodies, conduct research into various governmental actions, compile information base on the research, and
disclose the information to its members and to the general public.

"Political purpose" is defined in Commission Regulations 18215
and 18225 and means "made for the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election of a candidate or candidates, or the qualification
or passage of any measure." Copies of those regulations are included
for your review. Based on the information provided in your letter,
it appears that the receipts and expenditures of GEAR will not be
used for "political purpose", therefore, GEAR will not be required
to file campaign disclosure statements under the Political Reform Act.

I hope that this advice has been helpful. If you have any
further questions, please contact the Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Rich Eichman
Accounting Specialist

Enclosure

P.O. BOX 1293                    GOVERNMENTAL EDUCATION AND RESEARCH                    SANTA CRUZ, Ca., 95061


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: GEAR

Dear Sirs,

     Pursuant to Section 83114 of the California Government Code, the Governmental
Education and Research Association and N. Manfred Shaffer, its executive direc-
tor, a member of the association, hereby request an opinion as to whether it
or its members are required to file campaign statements or a statement of organi-
sation or both as a recipient committee, expenditure committee, or major donor
committee under its Political Reform Act of 1974.

     Governmental Education and Research is a non-profit association of business
and professional people in Santa Cruz County. The association was formed for the
purposes set forth in the Articles of Association, a copy of which is attached.
The purposes are generally to study local government issues, to report upon such
issues to the members of the association, to formulate positions on such issues,
and to inform the public of its position on such issues. There was some recent
discussion of the possibility of providing services to local candidates, but it
was determined not to do this.

     It is anticipated that the annual budget for the association will be in ex-
cess of $5,000 per year.

     Copies of a promotional brochure and a proposed newsletter which further
describe the intent of the association are attached hereto to indicate the con-
templated activities of the association.

     It is specifically intended that the representatives of the association
will attend meetings of government bodies, conduct research into various gov-
ernmental actions, compile information based on the research, and disclose the
information to its members and to the general public.

     As noted above, your opinion is requested as to whether the association
is, assuming that its actions are consistent with the aforementioned intent,
required to file campaign statements or a statement of organisation or both.
If any additional information is required, please feel free to contact me at
the following address: P.O. BOX 1293, Santa Cruz, Ca., 95061.


                                                    Sincerely yours,

                                                    [signature]
                                                    N. Manfred Shaffer

JG/pd
enc.

ARTICLES OF ASSOCIATION
OF
GOVERNMENTAL EDUCATION AND RESEARCH

The undersigned hereby form a non-profit association pursuant to the laws
of the State of California, and certify:

I
NAME

The name of the Association is "Governmental Education and Research".

II
PURPOSES

The Specific and primary purposes for which this Association is formed
are as follows:

(1) To identify, document and research critical issues of local concern.

(2) To perform research related to an understanding of local government
performance and management.

(3) To encourage individual and organizational participation in the
governmental process.

(4) To publish regular communications to be distributed to all members.

(5) To conduct seminars of benefit to members of the Association.

Additional purposes for which this Association is formed are as follows:

(1) To enter into, make, perform and carry out contracts of every kind
for any lawful purpose without limit as to amount, with any person, firm,
association, corporation, municipality, county, state or other governmental
agency.

(2) To have and to exercise all the powers conferred by laws of the
State of California upon associations as such law is now in effect or may
be at any time hereafter be amended.

III

MEMBERSHIP

Any person or organization is eligible formembership in this Association
upon payment of the annual membership subscription, any initiation fees,
and/or any monthly assessments as determined, from time to time, by the
Board of Directors.All memberships must be approved by the Board of Directors.

IV

BOARD OF DIRECTORS

This Association shall be governed by not more than twenty-five (25) nor
less than fifteen (15) directors. The Directors shall be elected by the
membership at the regular annual meeting of the Association. The duties
of the Board of Directors shall include, but not be limited to, the following:
(1) appointment of an Executive Committee, (2) adoption of By-Laws for the
Association, (3) adoption of an annual operating budget, (4) approval of
the contract for employment of an Executive Director.

V

EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE

The Executive Committee shall consist of five (5) members of the Board of
Directors who have been appointed by the Board. The Executive Committee
shall be responsible for the employment of an Executive Director who shall
manage the day to day affairs of the association. The Executive Director
shall report directly to the Executive Committee and shall meet on a regular
basis to review the activities of the Association.

VI

EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR

The Executive Director shall be responsible to carry out the purposes for
which this Association has been formed. All activities shall be within the
financial limitations imposed by the adopted annual budget. The executive
director shall be responsible for the employment of all personnel authorized
by the Executive Committee, shall enter into contracts for supply of required
materials necessary in the conduct of the business of the Association, and
shall fulfill all of the purposes enumerated in these articles or the
By-Laws of the Association.


Signed this date                           FOUNDING MEMBERS:
at Santa Cruz, California

What is Governmental Education and Research (GEAR)?
Since this is our first newsletter, we want to explain a bit about
our goals and objectives and what we hope to accomplish in the coming months.

If we are to succeed in our efforts to have local Government which is
responsible to all the residents of Santa Cruz, we cannot rely on the methods that we have used in the past. The "far left" and socialist factions in their desire to control this City and County have developed very
sophisticated and well organized strategies. If we are going to make our
views known, it is essential that we develop our own well organized efforts.

GEAR is the vehicle by which we can accomplish this. With your support, GEAR will make every effort to ensure that all governmental offices
are held by individuals who are responsive to the community needs.

GEAR intends to provide a wide variety of services, including:
* development and analysis of key issues and programs as they relate to
Santa Cruz
* a regular newsletter analyzing current political and economic affairs
* seminars to inform members of a wide variety of issues related to responsible government
* voter registration profiles
* identification and encouragement of as many volunteers as possible
to participate in the political process
* communication of the concerns of our membership to all candidates
* AND a continuing effort to keep all officeholders aware of the needs
of our members

WE STRONGLY BELIEVE THAT THE ONLY WAY RESPONSIBLE AND SENSIBLE LOCAL GOVERNMENT IS POSSIBLE IS THROUGH THE WILLINGNESS OF EVERYONE TO SUPPORT OUR
EFFORTS WITH MONEY, TIME, AND EXPERTISE. THE CAUSES WE ESPOUSE NEED OUR
HELP NOW!

GEAR is committed to an educational process, which will seek to inform all residents, particularly its members of proposed and pending legislation which could adversely affect or enhance its freedoms. A regular newsletter—GEAR-UP—will provide members with an update of local political and economic issues. Members may be provided with a wide variety of services to aid in the organizing and directing of an individual's participation in the governmental process.

Seminars will be conducted to make members aware of the mechanics for effective political participation.

MEMBERSHIP INFORMATION

Any person or organization is eligible to apply for membership in GEAR. A membership committee reviews all applications and makes recommendations to the Board of Directors. Four types of membership have been established—

Initial Annual
Fee Renewal

Sponsor $1,000 $500
Business 500 250
Patron 300 150
Subscriber (Individuals) 100 50

We invite you to participate.

Membership Application

Name

Firm Name (if applicable)

Address

Telephone

We wish to apply for a check in the amount of $_______ in GEAR.

Enclosed please find our provided affirm that we are willing to have our name will not be divulged to any person (other than members of the Board of Directors).

Governmental Education and Research
147 River St. South
Santa Cruz, Ca. 95060


Are you concerned that qualified people
who can represent you are encouraged to
become candidates for local political
offices? Are you concerned that they be
provided with all of the tools necessary to
ensure success in their efforts to win public
office? And are you concerned that they
continue to receive the advice and viewpoints
of people such as yourself? If so—you are
encouraged to participate in the activities
of GEAR.

GEAR has been formed to meet an unfulfilled
need in Santa Cruz for effective awareness
and action programs. The time for talk is
past—the time for action is now.

Our local city council and board of super-
visors are the major molding forces which
foster an economic and cultural climate for
our basic political, economic and social
institutions to flourish. For this reason it is
imperative that every business and profes-
sional person accept responsibility for
ensuring that policy making and service in
official positions of trust is in the hands of
individuals dedicated to the preservation
of democratic principles.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Governmental Education and Research Association (GEAR), a non-profit association focused on studying local government issues and informing the public, must file campaign statements or statements of organization under the Political Reform Act of 1974.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request document, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section present. Document includes supporting materials (Articles of Association, promotional brochure, newsletter excerpt). The 'old' format note indicates this is from 1981 and may have formatting variations.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from N. Manfred Shaffer, executive director of GEAR, a non-profit association in Santa Cruz County, asking the FPPC for an opinion on whether the association is required to file campaign statements or statements of organization under the Political Reform Act of 1974. The letter includes the association's Articles of Association and promotional materials describing GEAR's purposes and intended activities.
